mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, it signals a severe labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears and forces equity risk premium repricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand strength that boosts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving supply-side capacity utilization that supports near-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle expansion phases over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth that sustain consumer spending, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI inflation exceeding 0.6% monthly forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods following CPIAUCSL monthly spikes above 0.6%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction that destroys corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production outpacing its 12-month moving average signals robust manufacturing demand that supports earnings upgrades, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO outperformance yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of mid-cycle expansion observations.
